message:
feat(fm-parser): capture raw-format trivia and expose lens binding re-exports

Adds a `MermaidFormatComplement` aggregate that rides alongside the parsed IR
in `ParseResult::format_complement`, capturing the raw-format detail the IR
intentionally throws away:

- `line_ending` — `MermaidLineEndingStyle::{None, Lf, Crlf, Mixed}` describing
  what the source actually used. Mixed is its own variant so downstream tools
  can flag inconsistency rather than silently normalizing.
- `trailing_newline` — whether the source ends in a newline, so a rewrite
  round-trip does not gain or lose one.
- `whitespace` — per-span classification (`Indent`, `InterToken`, `Trailing`,
  `BlankLine`) with the original bytes preserved.
- `comments` / `directives` — raw text and spans for `%% ...` comments and
  directive blocks, which the IR strips during parsing.
- `quoted_literals` — each quoted run with its `MermaidQuoteStyle`
  (`Single` / `Double` / `Backtick`) so formatters and lens edits do not
  flip quote flavors.

`ir_builder::build_result()` now initializes the field to
`MermaidFormatComplement::default()` so existing paths keep compiling; the
actual population lives in the parser body and is wired per diagram type.

Also re-exports lens plumbing from `fm_core` at the crate root so consumers
(notably the wasm crate) have a single import site:

    MermaidLensBinding, MermaidLensEdit, MermaidLensEditResult,
    MermaidLensError, MermaidSourceMap, MermaidTextRange, Position, Span,
    apply_lens_edit, build_lens_bindings

These are the pieces needed to take a ParseResult, bind lenses against it,
and apply in-place text edits without going back through the IR.
